key_insight: Scratch testing is a fast way to assess adhesion and cohesive damage in thermally sprayed coatings, but its results are only meaningful when interpreted with coating microstructure, residual stress, and failure mode in mind.
engineering_relevance: The review helps engineers choose and standardize scratch-test parameters and critical-load criteria for qualifying thermal spray coatings used in high-wear, high-temperature, and corrosion-critical applications.
method: Reviews scratch-test methodologies and operational parameters
Correlates scratch response with microstructure, porosity, residual stress, and process route
Compares critical-load evaluation using microscopy, acoustic emission, and force sensing
evidence_signal: The abstract synthesizes findings across multiple thermal spray systems and processes, including APS, HVOF, HVAF, and D-gun, to compare scratch-induced damage and failure evolution.
watch_item: Scratch-test interpretation remains inconsistent across studies, so critical loads and failure criteria should not be transferred between coatings or setups without careful calibration.
paper_title: A critical analysis of the scratch test for thermally sprayed coatings
